more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Europe wants Putin’s oil and gas as Middle East goes implodes in inflames
more.description: Ukraine is facing mounting pressure from European Union leaders to allow an independent inspection of the damaged Druzhba pipeline, which transports Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia, the Financial Times has reported. The dispute follows accusations from Budapest and Bratislava that Kyiv is exaggerating the extent of damage caused by a Russian airstrike in January [...]The post Europe wants Putin’s oil and gas as Middle East goes implodes in inflames appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: US denies seeking Zimbabwe minerals in US$367m health deal
more.description: The United States has rejected claims that it sought access to Zimbabwe’s critical minerals under a proposed US$367 million health funding memorandum of understanding (MoU), stating that the agreement was strictly limited to public health cooperation. The clarification follows Zimbabwe’s decision to discontinue negotiations on the MoU, which had been framed under Washington’s America First [...]The post US denies seeking Zimbabwe minerals in US$367m health deal appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Brigadier General (Rtd) Hungwe declared national hero
more.description: Source: Brigadier General (Rtd) Hungwe declared national hero – herald George Maponga Masvingo Bureau President Mnangagwa has conferred national hero status on Brigadier General (Rtd) Jonathan Hungwe, who passed away on Friday at his farm in Mwenezi. more.title: Bureau Veritas blocks 200 million hazardous products from entering Zimbabwe
more.description: Source: Bureau Veritas blocks 200 million hazardous products from entering Zimbabwe – herald Business Reporter MORE than 200 million units of substandard and potentially life-threatening products have been intercepted and blocked from entering the Zimbabwean market, a senior official has revealed.
